summary.intent: Fix PR review comments on pull request #636 for the boulder-penalty branch, specifically addressing a Greptile concern about pre-configured redirect URIs bypassing loopback normalization.
summary.outcome: Extended loopback redirect URI normalization to also cover pre-configured URIs loaded from config files and environment variables, added a clarifying comment at the bypass guard, and pushed a follow-up commit to the open PR.

repo: OAuth redirect URI normalization lives in moltis_oauth::redirect::normalize_loopback_redirect, shared across mcp and provider-setup crates.

Built-in default redirect URIs (e.g. openai-codex uses http://localhost:1455/auth/callback) are already http, but user-supplied URIs from ~/.config/moltis/oauth_providers.json or MOLTIS_OAUTH_*_REDIRECT_URI env vars could be https://localhost which would break CallbackServer::wait_for_code port binding.
load_oauth_config reads from both a config file and environment variables, meaning normalization must cover both the registered and dynamically loaded paths.
The has_registered_redirect guard previously caused pre-configured URIs to skip normalization — this was the root of the Greptile P2 comment.

code.finding: Pre-configured redirect URIs (has_registered_redirect == true) bypassed normalize_loopback_redirect; fix applies normalization to loaded config as well and adds a clarifying comment at the guard.
Built-in OAuth provider defaults already use http://localhost URLs, so they are safe without normalization, but user-overridable paths are not.

workflow: Greptile provides automated P2-level PR review comments that can surface real correctness issues worth acting on.
YubiKey tap is required per global git config on every push.
Validation checklist before pushing: cargo test, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo +nightly fmt --check across all affected crates.

friction: The Greptile comment required reading multiple files (defaults.rs, lib.rs) to fully evaluate whether the bypass was safe, since the built-in values were fine but user-supplied config paths were not.
open_items: moltis-c26: investigate the manual [mcp.servers.*.oauth] override sub-complaint — could not reproduce without a live Attio connection; code path review suggests it is already wired correctly but needs live-environment validation.
